6.1.title: Anāśritaḥ karmaphalam — the true saṃnyāsī
6.1.body: Kṛṣṇa redefines saṃnyāsa: not अग्निहोत्र-त्याग, but doing कार्यं कर्म while अनाश्रितः कर्मफलम् — unattached to the fruit. अनाश्रितः is the past passive participle of आ + √श्रि (प्रथमा एकवचन, agreeing with the implicit कर्ता यः). The pair न निरग्निः न च अक्रियः negates the external markers and locates renunciation in inner stance.

6.1.title: Saṃnyāsī ca yogī ca — collapsing two paths
6.1.body: The double च in स संन्यासी च योगी च dissolves the chapter-5 question once more: the same person, by the same act, is both. The relative-correlative यः ... सः frame makes the criterion grammatical, not sociological — doer-of-action who is अनाश्रितः-of-phala is, by definition, both.

6.2.title: Saṃnyāsa = yoga; the saṅkalpa-renouncer alone is yogī
6.2.body: यं संन्यासम् इति प्राहुः योगं तं विद्धि — what they call संन्यास, know that as yoga; प्राहुः is लिट् of प्र + √ब्रू (प्रथम बहुवचन, 'they have said'). The negative half न हि असंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पः योगी भवति कश्चन makes the criterion explicit: the renunciation that matters is सङ्कल्प-त्याग, not action-त्याग.

6.2.title: Asaṃnyasta-saṅkalpa — the bahuvrīhi diagnostic
6.2.body: असंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पः is a bahuvrīhi: 'one by whom saṅkalpa is not renounced.' Kṛṣṇa supplies a sharp negative test — anyone still carrying saṅkalpa fails the योगी check, regardless of external posture. कश्चन universalises the negation: no exceptions.

6.3.title: Yogārūḍha defined — ascent then śama
6.3.body: Two stages, two means: आरुरुक्षोः मुनेः योगं कर्म कारणम् — for the muni still climbing, action is the means; योगारूढस्य तस्य एव शमः कारणम् — for the same one once ascended, stillness is. आरुरुक्षोः is षष्ठी एकवचन of the desiderative noun (one wishing to climb).

6.3.title: Tasya eva — same person, different stage
6.3.body: The एव in तस्य एव is the load-bearing word: not a different aspirant, the very same one. Kṛṣṇa thus blocks the reading that कर्मयोग and ध्यानयोग name two parties; they name two phases of one journey, and conflating their प्रathनs is the beginner's mistake.

6.4.title: Sarva-saṅkalpa-saṃnyāsī — the arrival test
6.4.body: यदा ... तदा gives the diagnostic: when one is no longer attached (न अनुषज्जते, लट् of अनु + √सञ्ज्) to इन्द्रियार्थ or to कर्म, then सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढः उच्यते. The arrival is named by what has *stopped* — saṅkalpa across both sense-objects and actions.

6.4.title: Indriyārtheṣu, karmasu — twin saptamīs of non-clinging
6.4.body: Two सप्तमी locatives — इन्द्रियार्थेषु and कर्मसु — mark the *fields* in which non-attachment must hold. The locative here is the सप्तमी of 'with respect to': it is not the world that must vanish, but the clinging in these two locations.

6.5.title: Ātmā as friend and foe — the reflexive verse
6.5.body: उद्धरेत् आत्मना आत्मानम् — 'one should lift the self by the self.' Three forms of आत्मन् in one pāda: तृतीया, द्वितीया, and the implicit nominative of the optional कर्ता. The विधिलिङ् उद्धरेत् (उत् + √हृ) is prescriptive, and न अवसादयेत् forbids the opposite — do not let the self sink the self.

6.5.title: Bandhuḥ ripuḥ ca ātmanaḥ — the genitive double-bind
6.5.body: आत्मा एव हि आत्मनः बन्धुः, आत्मा एव रिपुः आत्मनः — twice the षष्ठी (आत्मनः) flips the same noun into subject and object of friendship/enmity. एव isolates the agent: nothing external can be blamed once both relations originate within.

6.6.title: Yena ātmā ātmanā jitaḥ — the conquering self
6.6.body: बन्धुः आत्मा आत्मनः तस्य येन आत्मा एव आत्मना जितः — the self is friend to the one *by whom* the self has been conquered by the self. जितः is past passive participle (प्रथमा एकवचन agreeing with आत्मा); the तृतीया आत्मना names the instrument, the यः-clause names the conqueror.

6.6.title: Śatruvat — simile, not identity
6.6.body: अनात्मनः तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेत आत्मा एव शत्रुवत् — for the unconquered, the self conducts itself *like* an enemy (शत्रुवत्, the vati-suffix marking similitude). शत्रुत्वे is सप्तमी of state: 'in enmity.' The simile preserves ontology — the self has not become other, only acts as if.

6.7.title: Jitātmanaḥ — paramātmā is samāhitaḥ
6.7.body: जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः — for the one of conquered self and calmed mind, the supreme Self stands gathered. समाहितः is past passive participle of सम् + आ + √धा; the षष्ठी double (जितात्मनः, प्रशान्तस्य) names the locus of this composure.

6.7.title: Dvandvas of equality — heat/cold, honour/dishonour
6.7.body: शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः — सप्तमी plurals of the द्वन्द्व compounds locate where the यogī's samatā is tested. The locative here marks *occasions* of equality, not places; the pairs are deliberately paired (sensory + social) to cover both axes.

6.8.title: Jñāna-vijñāna-tṛptātmā — sated by knowing
6.8.body: ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा is a bahuvrīhi: 'one whose self is satisfied by jñāna and vijñāna.' The contrast with अन्न-tṛpti is the point — सत्तिsfaction has shifted source. कूटस्थः (immovable like an anvil) and विजितेन्द्रियः stack three qualifiers before युक्तः इति उच्यते.

6.8.title: Sama-loṣṭa-aśma-kāñcanaḥ — clod, stone, gold alike
6.8.body: समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः — a four-member bahuvrīhi: 'one for whom clod, stone, and gold are equal.' Three substances of escalating market value collapsed to one valuation. The compound itself enacts the leveling it describes.

6.9.title: Samabuddhi over seven kinds of people
6.9.body: सुहृत्-मित्र-अरि-उदासीन-मध्यस्थ-द्वेष्य-बन्धुषु साधुषु अपि च पापेषु — a nine-member सप्तमी plural cataloguing every social-moral relation. विशिष्यते (लट् passive of वि + √शिष्) — 'is distinguished/excels' — names the यogī by *what he sees the same*, not what he does.

6.9.title: Sādhuṣu api ca pāpeṣu — the hardest equality
6.9.body: The अपि in साधुषु अपि च पापेषु is load-bearing: equal sight even between virtuous and sinful, which is morally counter-intuitive. The verse closes the social ladder with the ethical ladder — the यogī's सम-दृष्टि must survive both.

6.10.title: Yogī yuñjīta — the ekākī injunction
6.10.body: योगी युञ्जीत सततम् आत्मानम् — विधिलिङ् of √युज् (आत्मनेपद, प्रथम एकवचन): 'let the yogī yoke the self constantly.' रहसि स्थितः, एकाकी, यतचित्तात्मा, निराशीः, अपरिग्रहः — five qualifiers stack the conditions: secret place, alone, restrained, expectation-less, non-acquisitive.

6.10.title: Nirāśīḥ aparigrahaḥ — the two negative seals
6.10.body: निराशीः (no expectation) and अपरिग्रहः (no acquisition) are both negative bahuvrīhis sealing the practice. आशीः is the standard 'wish/blessing'; the privative नि locks out future-leaning desire while अपरिग्रह locks out present-clutching possession.

6.11.title: Śucau deśe — establishing the seat
6.11.body: शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरम् आसनम् आत्मनः — सप्तमी एकवचन शुचौ देशे locates the where; प्रतिष्ठाप्य is the absolutive (causative of प्रति + √स्था): 'having set up.' The seat is आत्मनः — षष्ठी, 'his own.'

6.11.title: Cailājinakuśottaram — the three-layered seat
6.11.body: नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं — neither too high nor too low; चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् — a bahuvrīhi: 'having cloth, deerskin, and kuśa as upper covering' (read bottom-up: kuśa, deerskin, cloth). The very specificity is part of the pedagogy — yoga is bodily, not abstract.

6.12.title: Tatra ekāgraṃ manaḥ kṛtvā — the focusing absolutive
6.12.body: तत्र एकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा — 'there, having made the mind one-pointed': कृत्वा is absolutive of √कृ, prerequisite to the main verb युञ्ज्यात् (विधिलिङ् of √युज्). The participle यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः is a bahuvrīhi qualifying the unseen कर्ता.

6.12.title: Ātma-viśuddhaye — the dative of purpose
6.12.body: योगम् आत्मविशुद्धये — चतुर्थी एकवचन names the goal of the yoking: self-purification. The dative of purpose here re-frames meditation not as performance but as cleansing — the यoga is *for* a result, but the result is internal, not phalakāmya.

6.13.title: Samaṃ kāya-śiro-grīvam — the posture geometry
6.13.body: समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन् अचलं स्थिरः — three nouns in a samāhāra dvandva, held समम् (in line). धारयन् is शतृ-participle of causative √धृ ('holding'); अचलम् and स्थिरः double the immobility — one of the body, one of the person.

6.13.title: Saṃprekṣya nāsikāgram — gaze without scattering
6.13.body: सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वम् — absolutive of सम् + प्र + √ईक्ष्: 'having gazed at his own nose-tip.' Paired with दिशः च अनवलोकयन् — 'not looking at the directions' (शतृ negated by अन्). The gaze is anchored to forbid wandering.

6.14.title: Brahmacāri-vrate sthitaḥ — the celibate vow
6.14.body: प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीः ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः — three qualifying bahuvrīhis lead to स्थितः (PPP of √स्था) in the सप्तमी ब्रह्मचारिव्रते. The vow is the location, not the act — one *stands* in it.

6.14.title: Mac-citto yuktaḥ āsīta mat-paraḥ — Kṛṣṇa centring the meditation
6.14.body: मच्चित्तः ... आसीत मत्परः — आसीत is विधिलिङ् of √आस् (आत्मनेपद): 'let him sit.' Both मच्चित्तः and मत्परः are bahuvrīhis with first-person stem, making Kṛṣṇa the explicit object of the meditation, not an abstract brahman.

6.15.title: Yuñjann evaṃ sadā — the perpetual yoking
6.15.body: युञ्जन् एवं सदा आत्मानं योगी नियतमानसः — युञ्जन् is the शतृ-participle in continuous aspect ('yoking constantly'); नियतमानसः is the bahuvrīhi diagnostic. The verb of the sentence is अधिगच्छति (लट् of अधि + √गम्).

6.15.title: Nirvāṇa-paramāṃ mat-saṃsthām — the destination
6.15.body: शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थाम् अधिगच्छति — accusative chain of three objects of अधिगच्छति. निर्वाणपरमाम् is a bahuvrīhi ('having nirvāṇa as its highest'); मत्संस्थाम् locates that peace *in Kṛṣṇa*, again personalising the abstract.

6.16.title: Na atyaśnataḥ — the negation by ṣaṣṭhī
6.16.body: न अति-अश्नतः तु योगः अस्ति न च एकान्तम् अनश्नतः — षष्ठी एकवचन of शतृ-participles (अश्नतः = of one eating; अनश्नतः = of one not eating). The षष्ठी signals 'for/of': yoga *belongs* neither to the over-eater nor to the total faster.

6.16.title: Atisvapna-jāgrataḥ — sleep as the second axis
6.16.body: न च अति-स्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतः न एव — extremes of sleep are paired with extremes of food. The four-fold negation (over-eating, fasting, over-sleeping, sleeplessness) prepares the positive युक्त- string of the next verse.

6.17.title: Yukta-āhāra-vihārasya — the four yuktas
6.17.body: युक्ताहारविहारस्य ... युक्तचेष्टस्य ... युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य — three षष्ठी bahuvrīhis with युक्त (PPP of √युज्) as first member: regulated in food-and-movement, in action, in sleep-and-waking. Yoga belongs *to* such a person — the षष्ठी governs the absent भवति.

6.17.title: Yogo bhavati duḥkha-hā — yoga as sorrow-slayer
6.17.body: योगः भवति दुःखहा — दुःखहा is a tatpuruṣa with the suffix -हन् ('killer of'), प्रथमा एकवचन agreeing with योगः. The promise is functional: the regulated life produces a yoga that *kills* duḥkha, not merely manages it.

6.18.title: Viniyataṃ cittam ātmani — the locative of rest
6.18.body: यदा विनियतं चित्तम् आत्मनि एव अवतिष्ठते — विनियतम् (PPP of वि + नि + √यम्) qualifies चित्तम्; अवतिष्ठते is लट् आत्मनेपद of अव + √स्था ('settles down'). The सप्तमी आत्मनि names where the cittam comes to rest.

6.18.title: Niḥspṛhaḥ sarva-kāmebhyaḥ — the pañcamī of separation
6.18.body: निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यः — पञ्चमी बहुवचन of separation: 'free from all desires.' The pañcamī here is the case of 'away from,' so the अप-relation is grammatical, not metaphorical. युक्तः इति उच्यते तदा closes the यदा-तदा frame.

6.19.title: Dīpo nivāta-stho — the lamp-in-windless-place simile
6.19.body: यथा दीपः निवातस्थः न इङ्गते सा उपमा स्मृता — यथा introduces the upamāna; निवातस्थः is a tatpuruṣa ('standing in windless [place]'); न इङ्गते is लट् of √इङ्ग् ('does not waver'). The simile is *recorded* (स्मृता, PPP), suggesting traditional pedigree.

6.19.title: Yatacittasya yuñjato — the doubled ṣaṣṭhī
6.19.body: योगिनः यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतः योगम् आत्मनः — three षष्ठी genitives stacking ('of the yogī, of restrained mind, of one yoking') for whom the simile holds. The grammar enacts what the simile depicts: the citta gathered into one focused referent.

6.20.title: Yatra uparamate — the locative of cessation
6.20.body: यत्र उपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया — सप्तमी relative यत्र names the state, not a place; उपरमते is लट् आत्मनेपद of उप + √रम् ('comes to rest'); निरुद्धम् (PPP) qualifies चित्तम्. योगसेवया is तृतीया of means.

6.20.title: Ātmanā ātmānaṃ paśyann ātmani tuṣyati — three cases of self
6.20.body: आत्मना (तृतीया) आत्मानं (द्वितीया) पश्यन् आत्मनि (सप्तमी) तुष्यति — instrument, object, locus, all the same आत्मन्. The grammatical compression encodes the experience: seer, seen, and place of joy collapse into one referent.

6.21.title: Buddhi-grāhyam atīndriyam — beyond the senses
6.21.body: सुखम् आत्यन्तिकं यत् तत् बुद्धिग्राह्यम् अतीन्द्रियम् — the यत्-तत् correlative pins the bliss as 'graspable by बुद्धि, beyond the indriyas.' बुद्धिग्राह्यम् is a tatpuruṣa with the gerundive (-य) — 'to be grasped *by* बुद्धि' (तृतीया absorbed into the compound).

6.21.title: Sthitaś calati tattvataḥ — the immovable foothold
6.21.body: वेत्ति यत्र न च एव अयं स्थितः चलति तत्त्वतः — having known it, *standing there*, he does not waver तत्त्वतः ('in essence,' तसिल्-suffix turning the noun adverbial). The sthitaḥ ... na calati pair is the chapter-2 sthitaprajña echo, re-keyed to dhyāna.

6.22.title: Yaṃ labdhvā — the absolutive of acquisition
6.22.body: यं लब्ध्वा च अपरं लाभं मन्यते न अधिकं ततः — लब्ध्वा is absolutive of √लभ्; having attained *this*, he reckons no other gain higher than that (ततः, पञ्चमी ablative of comparison). The verse defines arrival by what no longer compares.

6.22.title: Guruṇāpi na vicālyate — the tṛtīyā of agent
6.22.body: यस्मिन् स्थितः न दुःखेन गुरुणा अपि विचाल्यते — सप्तमी यस्मिन् locates the standing; दुःखेन गुरुणा is तृतीया of the agent (passive construction: 'is not shaken *by* even a heavy duḥkha'). विचाल्यते is लट् passive of वि + √चल्.

6.23.title: Duḥkha-saṃyoga-viyogaṃ — yoga as disjunction-from-sorrow
6.23.body: तं विद्यात् दुःखसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम् — विद्यात् is विधिलिङ् of √विद्; the compound दुःखसंयोगवियोगम् ('disjunction from junction with sorrow') is a double-negative tatpuruṣa, structurally clever: viyoga of saṃyoga of duḥkha is itself called yoga.

6.23.title: Anirviṇṇa-cetasā — the negative bahuvrīhi of resolve
6.23.body: स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यः योगः अनिर्विण्णचेतसा — योक्तव्यः is gerundive of √युज् (प्रथमा एकवचन); अनिर्विण्णचेतसा is तृतीया of accompaniment — 'with an undejected mind.' The negative bahuvrīhi (अ + निर्विण्ण + चेतस्) prescribes the affective stance for practice.

6.24.title: Saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmān — abandonment by absolutive
6.24.body: सङ्कल्पप्रभवान् कामान् त्यक्त्वा सर्वान् अशेषतः — त्यक्त्वा is absolutive of √त्यज्; the compound सङ्कल्पप्रभवान् ('having saṅkalpa as origin') is a bahuvrīhi qualifying कामान्. The genealogy of desire is named — सङ्कल्प is the source, hence 6.2's saṅkalpa-renunciation.

6.24.title: Manasā indriya-grāmaṃ viniyamya — restrain by mind alone
6.24.body: मनसा एव इन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्ततः — तृतीया मनसा is the instrument and एव isolates it: by the mind *alone*, on all sides (समन्ततः). विनियम्य is absolutive of वि + नि + √यम्. The whole इन्द्रिय-group is taken as one referent (-ग्राम).

6.25.title: Śanaiḥ śanaiḥ uparamet — gradual cessation
6.25.body: शनैः शनैः उपरमेत् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया — विधिलिङ् of उप + √रम्; the reduplicated शनैः शनैः enforces gradualness. धृतिगृहीतया is तृतीया of the agent-instrument — 'by a buddhi held by dhṛti' (PPP गृहीतया from √ग्रह्).

6.25.title: Na kiñcid api cintayet — the empty thought
6.25.body: आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चित् अपि चिन्तयेत् — कृत्वा absolutive; आत्मसंस्थम् ('established in the self') qualifies मनः. The विधिलिङ् चिन्तयेत् with comprehensive negation (न ... किञ्चित् अपि) prescribes a content-less thinking, the meditative blank.

6.26.title: Yato yataḥ niścarati — the wherever clause
6.26.body: यतो यतः निश्चरति मनः चञ्चलम् अस्थिरम् — the doubled यतः (तसिल्-ablative-adverb) means 'from wheresoever'; the doubled ततः in the next pāda makes the structure 'from whatever direction it strays, from that direction'. निश्चरति is लट् of निः + √चर् ('strays out').

6.26.title: Ātmany eva vaśaṃ nayet — bring under control in the self
6.26.body: ततस्ततः नियम्य एतत् आत्मनि एव वशं नयेत् — नियम्य absolutive of नि + √यम्; नयेत् विधिलिङ् of √नी. The सप्तमी आत्मनि एव specifies *where* the mind is to be brought under control — not elsewhere, in the self alone.

6.27.title: Praśānta-manasaṃ — supreme bliss approaches him
6.27.body: प्रशान्तमनसं हि एनं योगिनं सुखम् उत्तमम् उपैति — द्वितीया एनं योगिनं is the *object* of approach; उपैति (लट् of उप + √इ) governs it. Reverse the English voice: bliss comes to him; he is not the seeker but the destination.

6.27.title: Brahma-bhūtam akalmaṣam — having become brahman
6.27.body: शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतम् अकल्मषम् — three accusative qualifiers of एनं योगिनम्: rajas-pacified, brahman-become, stain-less. ब्रह्मभूतम् (PPP of √भू with ब्रह्मन्) names the ontological shift; अकल्मषम् is the negative bahuvrīhi.

6.28.title: Yuñjann evaṃ sadā — second occurrence, framing the section
6.28.body: युञ्जन् एवं सदा आत्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः — the line repeats 6.15's युञ्जन् एवं frame to bracket the meditation section. विगतकल्मषः is a bahuvrīhi ('one whose stain has departed'), echoing अकल्मषम् of the previous verse.

6.28.title: Brahma-saṃsparśam atyantaṃ sukham — the unbroken touch
6.28.body: सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शम् अत्यन्तं सुखम् अश्नुते — अश्नुते is लट् आत्मनेपद of √अश् ('enjoys'); ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शम् is a tatpuruṣa accusative ('the touch of brahman'). The redoubled सुख — first तृतीया (instrument), then द्वितीया (object) — frames the result.

6.29.title: Sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānam — universal location
6.29.body: सर्वभूतस्थम् आत्मानं सर्वभूतानि च आत्मनि — द्वितीया आत्मानम् with the bahuvrīhi सर्वभूतस्थम् ('standing in all beings'); then the symmetric flip with सर्वभूतानि (द्वितीया plural) located आत्मनि (सप्तमी). Same content, two cases — that is the seeing.

6.29.title: Sarvatra samadarśanaḥ — equal sight everywhere
6.29.body: ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः — ईक्षते is लट् आत्मनेपद of √ईक्ष्; समदर्शनः is a bahuvrīhi ('one whose seeing is even'). सर्वत्र is the त्रल्-adverb of place (suffix -त्र, not -तस्). The verb is in the middle voice — the seeing turns back on the seer.

6.30.title: Yaḥ māṃ paśyati sarvatra — the reciprocal non-loss
6.30.body: यः मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति — द्वितीया माम् and सर्वम् name the objects of seeing; सप्तमी मयि locates the second. The यः-तस्य correlative carries through to the consequence.

6.30.title: Na praṇaśyāmi — Kṛṣṇa's first-person promise
6.30.body: तस्य अहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति — प्रणश्यामि is लट् उत्तम एकवचन of प्र + √नश्. The षष्ठी तस्य and मे frame the relation as mutual belonging; the negated 'I do not perish for him; he does not perish for me' is grammatically reciprocal.

6.31.title: Sarva-bhūta-sthitaṃ māṃ bhajati — worship of the in-dwelling
6.31.body: सर्वभूतस्थितं यः मां भजति एकत्वम् आस्थितः — द्वितीया माम् with the bahuvrīhi सर्वभूतस्थितम् ('established in all beings'); आस्थितः (PPP of आ + √स्था) qualifies the implicit सः and governs एकत्वम् as its object — 'standing in oneness.'

6.31.title: Sarvathā vartamāno 'pi — in any mode of life
6.31.body: सर्वथा वर्तमानः अपि स योगी मयि वर्तते — वर्तमानः is the शानच्-participle (आत्मनेपद present participle, stem in -मान) of √वृत्, 'while behaving in any way'; the अपि concessive grants total freedom of outward conduct. वर्तते is लट् आत्मनेपद of √वृत् — same root, doubled to make the point.

6.32.title: Ātmaupamyena sarvatra — by analogy to oneself
6.32.body: आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति यः अर्जुन — तृतीया औपम्येन is the means: 'by the analogy of self' (औपम्य is the abstract from उपमा). What is to be seen as 'same' is named in the next pāda.

6.32.title: Sukhaṃ vā yadi vā duḥkham — pleasure or pain alike
6.32.body: सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं स योगी परमः मतः — द्वितीया सुखम् and दुःखम् are the objects of the सर्वत्र समं पश्यति; वा यदि वा is the disjunctive 'whether ... or.' The यogī who applies *his own* sukha-duḥkha calibration outward is called *परमः* (mataḥ — PPP of √मन्).

6.33.title: Arjuna's challenge — sthitiṃ na paśyāmi
6.33.body: यः अयं योगः त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन — द्वितीया योगः with the PPP प्रोक्तः ('declared by you'); तृतीया साम्येन names the *quality* of the yoga (equality). मधुसूदन is सम्बोधन — not a participant in the action.

6.33.title: Cañcalatvāt sthiti-na — abstract noun in pañcamī
6.33.body: एतस्य अहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात् स्थितिं स्थिराम् — पञ्चमी चञ्चलत्वात् gives the cause ('because of fickleness'); the suffix -त्व abstracts the adjective into a noun. Arjuna names the obstacle in grammatically formal terms — and Kṛṣṇa will answer in kind in 6.35.

6.34.title: Cañcalaṃ pramāthi balavat dṛḍham — four-fold indictment
6.34.body: चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवत् दृढम् — four nominative adjectives stack against मनः: fickle, churning, strong, firm. The progression escalates — not just unsteady but actively disruptive (प्रमाथि), then strong, then *entrenched*.

6.34.title: Vāyor iva sudushkaram — like the wind
6.34.body: तस्य अहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोः इव सुदुष्करम् — षष्ठी तस्य ('of it [the mind]'); निग्रहम् द्वितीया as object of मन्ये (लट् of √मन्). The simile takes वायोः (षष्ठी) — 'as [the restraint] of the wind.' सुदुष्करम् doubles the prefix to push beyond just दुष्कर.

6.35.title: Abhyāsena vairāgyeṇa ca — the twin instruments
6.35.body: असंशयं महाबाहो मनः दुर्निग्रहं चलम् — Kṛṣṇa concedes Arjuna's diagnosis (असंशयम् = 'without doubt'). Then turns: अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते — तृतीया pair (अभ्यासेन, वैराग्येण) names the two means; गृह्यते is लट् passive of √ग्रह् ('it is grasped').

6.35.title: Tu — the pivot that answers 6.34
6.35.body: The तु in अभ्यासेन तु is the load-bearing word: 'yes, fickle, but —'. The verse models how a teacher answers a student's despair — grant the difficulty, then name the precise instruments. Two तृतीयाs — practice and dispassion — and the passive verb makes the mind itself the receiver, not the conqueror.

6.36.title: Asaṃyatātmanā — yoga unattainable by tṛtīyā
6.36.body: असंयतात्मना योगः दुष्प्रापः इति मे मतिः — तृतीया असंयतात्मना ('by one of unrestrained self') is the agent of the impossible attempt; दुष्प्रापः is a tatpuruṣa with the gerundive (दुस् + प्राप): 'hard to attain.'

6.36.title: Vaśyātmanā yatatā — by the restrained and striving
6.36.body: वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्यः अवाप्तुम् उपायतः — तृतीया pair वश्यात्मना यतता (present participle of √यत्, तृतीया एकवचन of यतत्- stem, 'by one striving'); शक्यः अवाप्तुम् is the infinitive of capability ('able to be attained'); उपायतः is the तसिल्-adverb 'by means.' The तु again pivots from impossibility to possibility, conditional on the agent.

6.37.title: Arjuna's follow-up — the failed striver
6.37.body: अयतिः श्रद्धया उपेतः योगात् चलितमानसः — three qualifiers stacked on the implicit subject: non-striving, endowed with faith (श्रद्धया तृतीया with उपेतः), and one whose mind has slipped from yoga (योगात् पञ्चमी of separation, with चलित- PPP).

6.37.title: Kāṃ gatiṃ gacchati — the destination question
6.37.body: अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति — अप्राप्य is the negative absolutive ('not having attained'); द्वितीया कां गतिम् ('what destination?') with गच्छति (लट् of √गम्). Arjuna's question is grammatically tight — interrogative pronoun in द्वितीया, verb of motion.

6.38.title: Chinnābhram iva — like a torn cloud
6.38.body: कच्चित् न उभयविभ्रष्टः छिन्नाभ्रम् इव नश्यति — कच्चित् marks anxious doubt; उभयविभ्रष्टः ('fallen from both') is a tatpuruṣa with PPP; छिन्नाभ्रम् ('torn cloud') is the upamāna with इव. नश्यति is लट् of √नश्.

6.38.title: Apratiṣṭho vimūḍho brahmaṇaḥ pathi — without footing on brahman's path
6.38.body: अप्रतिष्ठः महाबाहो विमूढः ब्रह्मणः पथि — three qualifiers: footing-less, bewildered, सप्तमी पथि locating the bewilderment 'on brahman's path' (ब्रह्मणः षष्ठी qualifying पथि). The fear is precise — drifting between worldly success and yogic arrival.

6.39.title: Chettum arhasi — the imperative of capability
6.39.body: एतत् मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुम् अर्हसि अशेषतः — द्वितीया एतत् संशयम् ('this doubt of mine'); छेत्तुम् is infinitive of √छिद् ('to cut'); अर्हसि is लट् मध्यम-पुरुष एकवचन of √अर्ह् ('you are fit/able to'). The infinitive + अर्ह construction is the standard 'you ought to.'

6.39.title: Tvad-anyaḥ — none other than you
6.39.body: त्वत् अन्यः संशयस्य अस्य छेत्ता न हि उपपद्यते — पञ्चमी त्वत् of comparison ('other than you'); षष्ठी संशयस्य ('of this doubt'); छेत्ता is the तृ-agent-noun ('cutter'). उपपद्यते is लट् passive-form of उप + √पद् — same verb as the 2.3 'fitting.'

6.40.title: Na iha na amutra — neither here nor there
6.40.body: पार्थ न एव इह न अमुत्र विनाशः तस्य विद्यते — the spatial-temporal pair इह/अमुत्र (here/hereafter) covers both worlds. विद्यते is लट् passive-form of √विद् ('exists/is found'); the षष्ठी तस्य names whose विनाश is absent.

6.40.title: Kalyāṇa-kṛt — the doer of good is safe
6.40.body: न हि कल्याणकृत् कश्चित् दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति — कल्याणकृत् is a tatpuruṣa ('good-doer,' with the agent-suffix क्विप्); द्वितीया दुर्गतिं is the object of गच्छति. तात ('dear one') marks Kṛṣṇa's tenderness in the line.

6.41.title: Puṇya-kṛtāṃ lokān — worlds of the merit-doers
6.41.body: प्राप्य पुण्यकृताम् लोकान् उषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः — two absolutives in sequence: प्राप्य (ल्यबन्त of प्र + √आप्) 'having attained,' उषित्वा (क्त्वान्त of √वस्) 'having dwelt.' षष्ठी पुण्यकृताम् qualifies लोकान्; द्वितीया plural शाश्वतीः समाः ('eternal years') is the duration accusative.

6.41.title: Śucīnāṃ śrīmatāṃ gehe — born in the pure and prosperous
6.41.body: शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टः अभिजायते — षष्ठी plural pair (शुचीनां श्रीमतां) qualifies गेहे (सप्तमी, 'in the house'); अभिजायते is लट् आत्मनेपद of अभि + √जन्. योगभ्रष्टः ('fallen from yoga,' tatpuruṣa with PPP) is the subject.

6.42.title: Yoginām eva kule — even rarer rebirth
6.42.body: अथवा योगिनाम् एव कुले भवति धीमताम् — षष्ठी plural double (योगिनां धीमताम्) qualifies सप्तमी कुले ('in the family'); the एव isolates योगिनाम् from the prior alternative (शुचीनां श्रीमताम्) and elevates it.

6.42.title: Durlabhataraṃ janma — the rarer birth
6.42.body: एतत् हि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यत् ईदृशम् — दुर्लभतरम् is the comparative (-तर) of दुर्लभ, qualifying जन्म; यत् ईदृशम् ('such as this') is the relative clause closing the sentence. The rarity is graded — not merely rare, *rarer*.

6.43.title: Pūrva-dehikaṃ buddhi-saṃyogam — the prior connection
6.43.body: तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम् — द्वितीया तम् बुद्धिसंयोगम् pauरvadehikam ('that connection-of-intellect, body-prior'); लभते is लट् आत्मनेपद of √लभ्. The तद्धित-suffix -इक turning पूर्वदेह into 'belonging to the previous body' is the technical point.

6.43.title: Yatate ca tato bhūyaḥ — strives again, more
6.43.body: यतते च ततः भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन — यतते लट् आत्मनेपद of √यत्; ततः (तसिल्-ablative-adverb 'from there'); भूयः is the comparative-adverb ('more'); सप्तमी संसिद्धौ ('toward perfection,' locative of goal). The rebirth resumes the practice mid-stride.

6.44.title: Pūrvābhyāsena hriyate — borne by prior practice
6.44.body: पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेन एव ह्रियते हि अवशः अपि सः — तृतीया पूर्वाभ्यासेन is the agent of the passive ह्रियते (लट् passive of √हृ, 'is carried away'); अवशः अपि ('even though without power') is concessive — the momentum overrides volition.

6.44.title: Śabda-brahma ativartate — even the inquirer transcends ritual
6.44.body: जिज्ञासुः अपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्म अतिवर्तते — जिज्ञासुः is the desiderative-agent of √ज्ञा ('one wishing to know'); षष्ठी योगस्य ('of yoga'); शब्दब्रह्म (द्वितीया) is the object of अतिवर्तते (लट् of अति + √वृत्). Even the mere *inquirer* surpasses the Vedic-ritual brahman.

6.45.title: Prayatnād yatamānaḥ — striving with effort
6.45.body: प्रयत्नात् यतमानः तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः — पञ्चमी प्रयत्नात् ('out of effort,' source-ablative); यतमानः is the शानच्-participle of √यत् (आत्मनेपद, 'striving'); the bahuvrīhi संशुद्धकिल्बिषः ('whose stains are well-cleansed,' PPP-based).

6.45.title: Aneka-janma-saṃsiddhaḥ — perfected over many births
6.45.body: अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धः ततः याति परां गतिम् — a tatpuruṣa with PPP ('perfected over many births'); याति लट् of √या; द्वितीया परां गतिम् ('the supreme destination'). The arc — many births, then arrival — answers Arjuna's 6.37 'kāṃ gatiṃ gacchati.'

6.46.title: Tapasvi-jñāni-karmi — three pañcamīs of comparison
6.46.body: तपस्विभ्यः अधिकः योगी ज्ञानिभ्यः अपि मतः अधिकः कर्मिभ्यः च अधिकः योगी — three पञ्चमी plurals (तपस्विभ्यः, ज्ञानिभ्यः, कर्मिभ्यः) as ablatives of comparison ('greater *than*'). अधिकः is the comparative-marker; मतः (PPP of √मन्) hedges with 'is held [to be].'

6.46.title: Tasmāt yogī bhava — the imperative conclusion
6.46.body: तस्मात् योगी भव अर्जुन — तस्मात् (पञ्चमी, 'from that') is the inferential 'therefore'; भव is लोट् मध्यम-पुरुष एकवचन of √भू. The verse compresses the three-way comparison into a single imperative addressed by name.

6.47.title: Mad-gatena antarātmanā — the inner self gone to me
6.47.body: योगिनाम् अपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेन अन्तरात्मना — षष्ठी plural योगिनां सर्वेषाम् ('of all yogīs even'); तृतीया मद्गतेन अन्तरात्मना ('with inner-self gone to me') as the instrument-accompaniment. मद्गतेन is the PPP of √गम् with first-person stem.

6.47.title: Yuktatamaḥ — the superlative seal of the chapter
6.47.body: श्रद्धावान् भजते यः मां स मे युक्ततमः मतः — श्रद्धावान् (मतुप्-suffix, 'possessing faith'); भजते लट् आत्मनेपद of √भज्. युक्ततमः is the superlative (-तम) of युक्त: 'most yoked.' The chapter that opened by collapsing सन्न्यासी and योगी closes by ranking even the यogīs and crowning the bhakta.
